100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On February 26, 1926, the League of Nations convened in Geneva to address escalating tensions in Europe, particularly concerning the ongoing conflict in the Ruhr Valley. Meanwhile, the United States continued to grapple with the repercussions of the Prohibition era, as debates intensified over the potential repeal of the 18th Amendment. In aviation news, Charles Lindbergh's historic transatlantic flight remained a topic of fascination, inspiring ongoing discussions about the future of air travel.
Updated: Thursday, February 26, 2026 at 06:31:58 AM CST
